A reminderA “class type” is a struct, class, or union type. Although we’ll be demonstrating “class templates” on structs for simplicity, everything here applies equally well to classes.
A “class type” is a struct, class, or union type. Although we’ll be demonstrating “class templates” on structs for simplicity, everything here applies equally well to classes.

For advanced readersThe above example makes use of a feature called class template specialization (covered in future lesson26.4 -- Class template specialization). Knowledge of how this feature works is not required at this point.
